custom.name: auto-session4-no-silent-build
custom.message: Learned from session 4: Silent build failures hide missing tools. Let builds fail loudly or check at runtime with which.

custom.name: auto-session4-pydantic-schema-sync
custom.message: Learned from session 4: Verify Pydantic response schemas include all ORM model fields. Missing extracted_path hid the action buttons.

custom.name: auto-session5-no-scalar-one-or-none-on-fk
custom.message: Learned from session 5: scalar_one_or_none() crashes with MultipleResultsFound on one-to-many relations. Use .order_by().limit(1) or .all() instead.

custom.name: auto-session5-yara-no-unreferenced-strings
custom.message: Learned from session 5: Every YARA string must appear in the condition. Unreferenced strings cause compilation errors.

custom.name: auto-review-no-raw-join-in-sandbox
custom.message: Learned from review S13: Use validate_path() instead of os.path.join for paths under firmware root. Raw join follows symlinks without sandbox checks.

custom.name: auto-review-no-shell-interpolation
custom.message: Learned from review S2/S4: Use shlex.quote() for all variables interpolated into shell commands. Prefer create_subprocess_exec (list form) over sh -c.

custom.name: auto-review-no-commit-in-tool-handler
custom.message: Learned from review K1: Use context.db.flush() in tool handlers. The outer MCP dispatch handles commit — committing here prevents rollback on partial failures.

custom.name: auto-standalone-dockerignore-venv
custom.message: Learned from standalone binary campaign: Ensure .dockerignore excludes .venv -- COPY . . will overwrite the Docker-built venv with the host venv, causing Python version mismatches and missing modules.

custom.name: auto-standalone-lief-enum-case
custom.message: Learned from standalone binary campaign: LIEF Mach-O CPU_TYPE enums are UPPERCASE (X86_64, ARM64), not lowercase. Always verify with dir().

custom.name: auto-session19-venv-which
custom.message: Learned from session 19: In Docker with uv/venv, CLI tools from pip packages land in /app/.venv/bin/ not system PATH. Use: shutil.which(tool) or shutil.which(tool, path="/app/.venv/bin")

custom.name: auto-docker-opt-verify-ldd
custom.message: Learned from docker-image-optimization: Verify runtime deps with ldd before trusting comments about which packages are needed. Comments rot faster than code.

custom.name: auto-fwid-api-function-missing-firmwareId
custom.message: Learned from S37 firmware-id bug: Every file API function must accept firmwareId param since the backend resolve_firmware dependency uses it for scoping.

custom.name: auto-s37-case-insensitive-fnmatch
custom.message: Learned from S37: User-facing file search should be case-insensitive. Use fnmatch.fnmatch(name.lower(), pattern.lower()) instead.

custom.name: auto-extraction-roots-no-direct-extracted-path
custom.message: Learned from feature-extraction-integrity (CLAUDE.md rule 16): Filesystem walks MUST use get_detection_roots(firmware) from app/services/firmware_paths.py - firmware.extracted_path is ONE rootfs and misses scatter-zip siblings, multi-archive containers, and nested unblob output. Per-binary flows (emulation/fuzzing/device/sandbox) may still use extracted_path. Originally uncovered by DPCS10 md1dsp.img bug + RespArray ZIP regression.

custom.name: auto-classifier-magic-offset-beyond-buffer
custom.message: Learned from feature-classifier-patterns-mcu-kernel: The detector reads only detector._MAGIC_READ_BYTES (64) bytes into `magic`. Indexing past 0x40 (64) silently fails to match. If a new format needs a deeper header check (e.g. iMX-RT BOOT_DATA @ 0x1000), widen _MAGIC_READ_BYTES with a rationale OR route through a per-format parser in parsers/, do not add an unreachable index.

custom.name: auto-intake-sweep-1-no-stat-docker-sock
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep Phase 1 (commit bac49ea, /health/deep regression 29dba35): stat-based /var/run/docker.sock presence checks fail after the docker-socket-proxy migration because the raw socket is no longer mounted. Probe via get_docker_client().containers.list(limit=1) instead -- verifies BOTH DOCKER_HOST connectivity AND the proxy CONTAINERS allowlist.

custom.name: auto-intake-sweep-1-no-docker-from-env
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep Phase 1 Stream D (commit bac49ea -- 10 sites across 8 files migrated): use get_docker_client() from app.utils.docker_client instead of docker.from_env(). The factory honours settings.docker_host which routes through the socket proxy; direct from_env() bypasses the proxy and breaks emulation/fuzzing/terminal flows once the raw socket mount is removed.

custom.name: auto-wave3-pydantic-extra-ignore-on-api-schemas
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep Wave 3 stream beta (DeviceInfo BROM bug): Pydantic extra='ignore' on API-boundary schemas silently strips fields sent by the service layer -- DeviceInfo dropped 4 BROM fields, surfacing as (dev as any).mode === undefined at runtime with zero compile-time signal. Prefer extra='forbid' (loud drift) or explicit field declarations. Use extra='allow' only for ingestion DTOs with a documented reason.

custom.name: auto-wave3-frontend-tsc-no-noemit
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep Waves 1+2+3 (CLAUDE.md Rule #24): wairz frontend tsconfig uses files: [] + project references; tsc --noEmit exits 0 silently without descending into referenced projects (Rule #17 silent-CLI-exit instance). Use npx tsc -b --force. Rule-17 canary mandatory once per session before trusting any green typecheck output: write a known-bad .ts file, run the command, confirm it fails.

custom.name: auto-wave3-intake-yaml-status-lowercase
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep Wave 3 stream gamma: intake YAML status: values should be lowercase (completed, pending, partial, draft, active) for close-out query coherence. Capitalised values work but break case-sensitive greps (grep -l 'status: completed' misses capital-C entries; close-out queries must use grep -li). Status-bump should land in the same commit that ships the intake's work, not wait for a maintenance-sweep.

custom.name: auto-fleet-worktree-requires-worktree-add
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep sessions 435cb5c2+198243b8 (CLAUDE.md Rule #23 refined by Wave-3 evidence): git checkout -b feat/{branch} ALONE is insufficient under shared on-disk checkout -- alpha and gamma saw 4 cross-stream commit-sweep incidents across 14 commits when they only used branch-checkout. Beta had 0 sweeps in 4 commits AFTER running git worktree add .worktrees/stream-{name} -b feat/stream-{name}-{date} and operating inside that path. Each parallel sub-agent prompt MUST issue git worktree add (not just git checkout -b) and operate inside .worktrees/<stream>/. The Fleet harness isolation: worktree sentinel is a no-op at the working-tree level.

custom.name: auto-phase5-no-private-scan-cross-module
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep session 93a4948d Phase 5 (backend-private-api-and-circular-imports P1): do not import the private _scan_credentials / _scan_crypto_material / _scan_shadow helpers from security_audit_service across module boundaries. Use the public dispatcher: `from app.services.security_audit_service import run_scan_subset; run_scan_subset(root, ['credentials','crypto_material','shadow'], findings)`. The private helpers are intentionally internal; the public API hides signature changes and the SCANNERS registry keeps the set of available checks single-sourced.

custom.name: auto-phase5-cache-use-services-cache-helpers
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep session 93a4948d Phase 5 (backend-cache-module-extraction-and-ttl): use app.services._cache helpers (store_cached / get_cached / exists_cached / invalidate_firmware / cleanup_older_than) instead of hand-rolling the delete-then-insert pattern against AnalysisCache. The helpers are the canonical path — they handle firmware-wide (binary_sha256 IS NULL) keys, issue flush() so callers own commit(), and their delete-then-insert is idempotent without relying on the unique constraint. Exemptions: app/services/_cache.py itself, app/services/export_service.py and app/services/import_service.py (legitimate bulk patterns), test files.

custom.name: auto-frontend-rebuild-not-restart
custom.message: Learned from intake-sweep session 93a4948d (CLAUDE.md Rule #26): `docker compose restart frontend` reuses the stale image — the baked-in `/usr/share/nginx/html/dist` does NOT pick up code changes under `frontend/src/**`. Use `docker compose up -d --build frontend` instead. Session 93a4948d incident: a TypeError thrown in Sidebar.tsx from `projects.map(...)` traced to a frontend container built before Zeta's `unwrap()` helper landed; `projectStore.fetchProjects` was getting `{items:[...]}` from the new backend Page-envelope response, but the pre-Zeta bundle returned that raw to the store. Companion to Rule #8 (backend+worker rebuild discipline).

custom.name: auto-frontend-fake-scan-failed-error
custom.pattern: errors:\s*\[\s*['\"](Scan failed|Security audit failed|YARA scan failed)['\"]\s*\]
custom.message: Learned from session 93a4948d (CLAUDE.md Rule #2x bug-fix cluster): hardcoded fake-error arrays like `errors: ['Scan failed']` inside a `catch {}` block discard the real exception. Replace with `errors: [extractErrorMessage(e, 'Operation failed')]` and bind the exception with `catch (e) { ... }`. The backend is often succeeding while the UI claims failure — root cause is almost always a swallowed axios timeout from the default 30s on a long-running scan/audit endpoint. See .planning/knowledge/wairz-intake-sweep-bug-fixes-2026-04-19-antipatterns.md anti-pattern #1.

custom.name: auto-frontend-long-op-no-explicit-timeout
custom.message: Learned from session 7e8dd7c3 (bug.har complaint + generateSbom timeout + full sweep commit 6814461..ce4bf45): long-running backend calls must declare an explicit timeout: option on the options object — default axios timeout is 30s (client.ts) and these endpoints routinely exceed it, surfacing a fake 'scan failed' to the UI while the backend completes successfully. Common tiers: SECURITY_SCAN_TIMEOUT=600_000 (scans, SBOM, audit, fuzzing analyze/triage, MCP tools, uploads), GHIDRA_ANALYSIS_TIMEOUT=180_000 (decompile, cleaned-code), RADARE2_ANALYSIS_TIMEOUT=90_000 (functions, imports, disasm, binary-info), DEVICE_BRIDGE_TIMEOUT=300_000 (device import/dump). Use the matching constant already defined in the sibling file (findings.ts / sbom.ts pattern). Prior rule defects: (a) verb allowlist missed generate/triage/analyze/capture/decompile/bytecode/sast/cleaned-code/disasm/network-analysis/tools-run; (b) no negative-lookahead check — fired on already-annotated calls. Both fixed.

custom.name: auto-phase5-no-security-audit-service-import
custom.message: Learned from campaign wairz-intake-sweep-2026-04-19 session b56eb487: security_audit_service.py was deleted in Phase 5 part 2 split (commit fb28072); module no longer exists. Use from app.services.security_audit import ... instead.

custom.name: auto-phase5-no-manifest-checks-mixin
custom.message: Learned from campaign wairz-intake-sweep-2026-04-19 session b56eb487: ManifestChecksMixin was removed in Phase 5 part 1 split (commit 1577eaa); inheritance replaced with composition via ManifestChecker. Use from app.services.manifest_checks import ManifestChecker instead.

custom.name: auto-phase5-no-sbom-service-import
custom.message: Learned from campaign wairz-intake-sweep-2026-04-19 session 7e8dd7c3: sbom_service.py was deleted in Phase 5 part 3 split (commit b29ef8f); module no longer exists. Use from app.services.sbom import ... instead (SbomService, IdentifiedComponent, CPE_VENDOR_MAP, etc. are all re-exported from the package __init__.py). Strategy classes live under app.services.sbom.strategies.*

custom.name: auto-phase5-no-emulation-service-import
custom.message: Learned from campaign wairz-intake-sweep-2026-04-19 session 7e8dd7c3: emulation_service.py was deleted in Phase 5 part 5 split (commit c267931); module no longer exists. Use from app.services.emulation import EmulationService instead. Sub-modules (docker_ops, kernel_selection, sysroot_mount, user_mode, system_mode) can be imported directly if needed — e.g. from app.services.emulation.docker_ops import copy_dir_to_container for free-function access.

custom.name: auto-phase5-no-mobsfscan-service-import
custom.message: Learned from campaign wairz-intake-sweep-2026-04-19 session 7e8dd7c3: mobsfscan_service.py was deleted in Phase 5 part 4 split (commit 206fbd5); module no longer exists. Use from app.services.mobsfscan import ... instead (MobsfScanPipeline, MobsfScanFinding, get_mobsfscan_pipeline, mobsfscan_available, normalize_mobsfscan_findings, SUPPRESSED_PATH_PATTERNS, SUPPRESSED_RULES all re-exported).

custom.name: auto-frontend-multipart-no-explicit-timeout
custom.message: Multipart upload endpoints must declare a timeout. File uploads up to MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE_MB (default 2 GB) can take several minutes on slow links; the default axios 30s fires mid-upload surfacing a fake 'upload failed'. Use UPLOAD_TIMEOUT=600_000 (10 min) per the firmware.ts / documents.ts / kernels.ts pattern. Learned from session 7e8dd7c3 long-op timeout sweep.

custom.name: auto-q4-no-get-analysis-cache
custom.message: Learned from session 480666ce (Q4): GhidraAnalysisCache class + get_analysis_cache() singleton removed in commit a7bfe47. Use module-level ghidra_service.X() directly — e.g. await ghidra_service.get_functions(...). For tests, patch the module binding: patch('module.ghidra_service', mock_cache) with positional 2nd arg, not return_value=.

custom.name: auto-module-level-asyncio-lock
custom.message: Module-level asyncio.Lock() construction runs at import time, before any event loop. On workers / mcp_server / strict-async contexts this can raise 'no running event loop'. Safe pattern: declare `_lock: asyncio.Lock | None = None` + helper `def _get_lock(): global _lock; if _lock is None: _lock = asyncio.Lock(); return _lock`. Learned from session 480666ce Q4 Ghidra singleton drop — see ghidra_service.py:202-216.

custom.name: auto-ci-pytest-ignore-requires-intake
custom.message: CI pytest --ignore= flags hide real signal. Each ignored file MUST have a tracking intake in .planning/intake/*-unstable-*.md with per-file failure triage (test-bug / product-bug / missing-fixture / deprecated). Remove the --ignore= line in the same commit that makes the file green. Learned from session 480666ce backend-tests.yml workflow — see .planning/intake/backend-pytest-unstable-tests.md.
